note: FICTIONAL paraphrased analogues of the kinds of rules a real coding standard contains. NOT the Australian Coding Standards (ACS), which are copyright IHACPA. Wording is invented for testing.

rules.title: Principal diagnosis
rules.text: The principal diagnosis is the condition established after study to be chiefly responsible for occasioning the patient's episode of admitted care. Where the reason for admission is investigated and a definitive condition is found, that condition is the principal diagnosis.

rules.title: Additional diagnoses
rules.text: Code an additional diagnosis only when the condition meets at least one criterion during the episode: it requires treatment; requires diagnostic evaluation; requires increased nursing care or monitoring; or extends the length of stay. Conditions documented but not meeting any criterion are not coded.

rules.title: Symptoms versus definitive diagnosis
rules.text: Do not assign a symptom or sign code as the principal diagnosis when a related definitive diagnosis has been established and documented for the same episode. Code the definitive diagnosis instead.

rules.title: Condition onset flag
rules.text: Assign a condition onset flag to every diagnosis: flag 1 = condition present on admission; flag 2 = condition arose during the episode of admitted care (hospital-acquired).

rules.title: Code also / multiple coding
rules.text: Where a code carries a 'code also' instruction, assign the additional code(s) named when the related condition is documented and clinically relevant to the episode.

rules.title: Negated and ruled-out conditions
rules.text: Do not code a condition that is documented as absent, negated, or ruled out (e.g. 'no evidence of', 'denies', 'ruled out'). A negated finding is not a codeable diagnosis.

rules.title: Historical conditions
rules.text: Do not code a past/resolved condition documented only as history (e.g. 'history of', 'previous', 'ex-') unless it is being actively treated or investigated, or otherwise affects care, this episode.

rules.title: Uncertain diagnoses
rules.text: For an inpatient episode, a diagnosis documented at separation as 'probable', 'likely' or 'suspected' and treated as such may be coded as if established. A diagnosis merely queried and not treated is not coded; code the presenting symptom instead.

rules.title: Family history
rules.text: Do not code a condition attributed to a family member. Family history is coded only with a dedicated family-history code when it affects the current episode's management.

rules.title: Morphology of neoplasms
rules.text: When coding a neoplasm, also assign the morphology code that describes the histological type.

rules.title: Dagger and asterisk (dual) coding
rules.text: For a manifestation coded with the dagger/asterisk convention, assign the dagger (underlying condition) code first and the asterisk (manifestation) code second. An asterisk code is never a principal diagnosis.

rules.title: Combination codes
rules.text: Where a single combination code fully describes two conditions (or a condition and its complication), assign the combination code instead of coding the components separately.

rules.title: Validity edits
rules.text: A coded episode must pass validity edits: the principal diagnosis must be an acceptable principal (not a symptom/asterisk code where a definitive code exists); sex-specific codes must match patient sex; and mutually excluded codes must not co-occur.
